rxn_class_summary: Visible-light-promoted intramolecular [2+2] cycloaddition of indole-tethered terminal olefins via energy transfer to give cyclobutane-fused angular tetracyclic spiroindolines.

key_intermediates.name: Triplet excited state of indole substrate (1a-T1, 56.0 kcal/mol)

electron_flow: Photoexcited Ir-photosensitizer V transfers triplet energy to indole substrate 1a, populating its T1 state (single electrons at C2/C3 of indole); the C3 (or C2) radical attacks the tethered terminal olefin in a tail-to-tail fashion to give a triplet 1,4-biradical, which crosses to an open-shell singlet via MECP and closes by radical-radical combination to the cyclobutane-fused spiroindoline.
rate_determining_step: Tail-to-tail C–C bond formation via TS1 (12.7 kcal/mol barrier relative to 1a-T1).

probe_reagents.name: 2,5-dimethylhexa-2,4-diene
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; standard reaction significantly inhibited (2a obtained in only 26% yield), supporting triplet intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Stern–Volmer quenching with substrate 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Linear quenching of photoexcited V luminescence by 1a (R^2 = 0.95), confirming excited-state interaction.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a
probe_reagents.observation: E1/2(ox) = +1.16 V, E1/2(red) = -0.44 V vs Ag/AgCl; no correlation between sensitizer redox potentials and reactivity, disfavoring SET mechanism.

notes: Favored pathway is triplet energy transfer with tail-to-tail cyclization via TS1 (68.7 kcal/mol) giving spiro[4.4] biradical INT1, then ISC through MECP1 to open-shell singlet INT1-OSS, then radical–radical combination (TS2, 59.1 kcal/mol) to product 2a. Alternative head-to-head pathway via TS1' (72.9 kcal/mol) leading to bridged [3.1.1] iso-2a (52.3 kcal/mol) is computed but not observed; head-to-tail TS could not be located. SET (photoinduced single-electron transfer) mechanism was considered but ruled out because reactivity does not correlate with photosensitizer redox potentials. Thermal [2+2] (TS3, 80.7 kcal/mol) is prohibited. ΔG(T1–S0) of substrate vs sensitizer correlates with yields, further supporting energy-transfer mechanism. X-ray structures of derivatives 6 and 8 confirm product connectivity.
